title: 3 Warren Buffett Stocks to Hold Forever
text: E-commerce behemoth Amazon's sheer size makes keeps it capable of fending off competitors. Mastercard shares aren't cheap.

title: The 7 Highest-Yielding Dividend Gems in Warren Buffet's Crown
text: Warren Buffett doesn't hide the fact he loves dividends. While he doesn't buy a business solely because it pays a dividend he enjoys when companies pay one and regularly increase it.

title: Rising Only Half The S&P's Gain In 2023, Where Is Citigroup Stock Headed?
text: Citigroup's stock (NYSE: C) has gained 12% YTD, as compared to the 24% rise in the S&P500 over the same period. Further, the stock is currently trading at $51 per share, which is 8% below its fair value of $56 – Trefis' estimate for Citigroup's valuation.

title: Buffett's 4 Dividend Buys For December
text: The article discusses the dividend-paying stocks in Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway portfolio. It highlights three stocks - The Kraft Heinz Co, Ally Financial Inc, and HP Inc - that have annual dividends exceeding their single share prices. The article also provides projected net gains for ten top Buffett-held dividend stocks by December 2024.

title: Earnings Calendar Spotlight: 4 Red-Hot Financial Stocks To Watch As Q4 Earnings Season Looms
text: The earnings calendar is barren until the end of the year, but earnings aren't far off for leaders like JPMorgan and Wells Fargo.

title: Citi Appointed as Depositary Bank for ZKH Group Limited ADR Program
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Citi's Issuer Services, acting through Citibank, N.A., has been appointed as the depositary bank by ZKH Group Limited (“ZKH”), a leading maintenance, repair and operations ("MRO") procurement service platform in China, to act as Depositary Bank for its American Depositary Receipt (“ADR”) program. ZKH's American Depositary Shares (“ADSs”) trade on the NYSE under the ticker “ZKH”. Each ADS represents thirty-five (35) Class A ordinary shares of ZKH. The initial public of.

title: Citigroup to close distressed-debt desk as bank's overhaul continues: reports
text: Citigroup Inc. is reportedly closing its distressed-debt business as the latest part of Chief Executive Jane Fraser's overhaul of the major Wall Street bank.

title: Citigroup to shut global distressed-debt business - CNBC
text: Dec 20 (Reuters) - U.S. lender Citigroup (C.N) is closing its global distressed-debt business as part of CEO Jane Fraser's overhaul, CNBC reported on Wednesday citing people with direct knowledge of the matter.

title: Citigroup to close global distressed-debt business as part of CEO Jane Fraser's overhaul
text: Citigroup is exiting businesses with poor returns to bolster the bank's odds of hitting Fraser's performance targets.

title: This is a good time to invest in a bond fund and this manager has an advantage over his largest competitors
text: Long-term interest rates have been declining rapidly over the past two months. The timing is still good for income-seeking investors to buy shares of bond funds and one excellent example is the ICON Flexible Bond Fund, which is managed by Jerry Paul.

title: Citigroup: Macro May Be Setting The Stage For A Monster Rally In 2024
text: Bank stocks are cheap and could see higher multiples with rate cuts and a manageable credit environment. Citigroup has lately been making good progress in restructuring its operations, setting up for an expanding cost-to-income ratio going into 2024 and beyond. Citi now projects a 4-5% CAGR revenue growth goal through 2026, amounting to $87 billion to $92 billion in revenues.

title: Citigroup employees, on edge over layoffs, told they can work remotely until the new year
text: Citigroup employees worried about CEO Jane Fraser's sweeping overhaul expressed concern over whether their jobs will exist in the new year.

title: Citigroup to end municipal underwriting and market-making division
text: Citigroup Inc (NYSE:C) is shutting down its municipal underwriting and market-making division, according to an internal memo, Reuters reported. “The economics of these activities are no longer viable given our commitment to increase the firm's overall returns,” the memo said.

title: Citigroup shutting down municipal bond trading unit: WSJ
text: Citigroup Inc.'s stock C, +1.82% was up by 0.7% in premarket trading on Friday after the bank said in an internal memo it's shutting down its municipal underwriting and trading units by March 31 of 2024. Most of Citi's municipal sales, trading and banking colleagues are exiting the bank, according to the memo, which was provided to The Wall Street Journal.

title: Citigroup closes municipal underwriting and market-making unit- memo
text: Citigroup has decided to close its municipal underwriting and market-making activities, according to a memo seen by Reuters.

title: Bond prices rally for JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs and Bank of America as sentiment turns bullish on money-center banks
text: Corporate bonds from the big money-center banks are rallying in concert with their stock prices amid more bullish sentiment on Wall Street about potential interest-rate cuts in 2024.

title: Why Are Bank Stocks Up Today?
text: Bank stocks are up today and investors wonder why have come to the right place! The reason that bank stocks are up on Thursday has to do with an update from the Federal Reserve.

title: Dividend Harvesting Portfolio Week 145: $14,500 Allocated, $1,274.81 In Projected Dividends
text: The markets had a strong week fueled by optimistic economic data, with the S&P 500 increasing by 1.2% and the Dow Jones climbing by 2.41%. The Dividend Harvesting Portfolio finished up 5.17% on invested capital, generating $17.17 in dividend income in week 145. The portfolio is positioned well for the future, with a focus on generating ongoing dividend income while mitigating downside risk.

title: Citi reportedly offers partial early bonuses to encourage exits amid massive overhaul
text: The news comes at a time when the bank is undertaking a sweeping reorganization that is expected to be complete by the end of the first quarter of next year.

title: Citigroup offers partial early bonuses to encourage departures - Bloomberg News
text: Citigroup is offering to pay some of its employees a portion of their annual bonuses early if they agree to voluntarily depart, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

title: 3 Bank Stocks I Wouldn't Touch With a 10-Foot Pole
text: A new report from investment bank Goldman Sachs (NYSE: GS ) claims that hedge funds are dumping bank stocks heading into year's end. According to Goldman, hedge funds sold bank and financial related stocks for a 10th consecutive week through Dec. 8.

title: The 3 Most Undervalued Blue-Chip Stocks to Buy in December
text: At a glance, the market might appear overvalued as the S&P 500 approaches 2023 highs. However, in the past year, the performance has been quite bifurcated, with the Magnificent 7 soaring while most of the other 493 stocks have lagged.

title: Citi Provides Investment and Technology to Colombian FinTech Supra
text: Citi has led a strategic investment round in Supra, a Colombian FinTech that enables cross-border payments and treasury solutions. Supra provides these solutions to small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) involved in import and export activities, Citi said in a Monday (Dec. 11) press release.

title: Citi Leads Strategic Investment Round in Colombian Fintech Supra
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Citi has led a strategic investment round in Supra. Far Out Ventures and H20 Capital also participated in the financing round. Supra is a Colombian fintech that enables cross-border payments and treasury solutions for small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs) that participate in import and export activities. The new capital will enable the growth of Supra's Colombian operations to fulfil its payment aggregator role in partnership with Foreign Exchange Market Intermedia.

title: Ember Completes Take Private of H2O Innovation
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Ember Infrastructure Management, LP (“Ember”) announces today the completion of the previously announced acquisition of H2O Innovation Inc. (“H2O Innovation” or the “Company”) by an entity controlled by funds and vehicles managed by Ember, with the participation of Ontario Power Generation Inc. Pension Fund (“OPG”), Investissement Québec (“IQ”), Caisse de dépôt et placement du Québec (“CDPQ”) and key executives of the Company, for a purchase price of $C395 million. “C.

title: Citigroup: Mark Mason's Path To ROTCE Of 11% - 12%
text: Mark Mason, Citigroup CFO, recently updated 2023 earnings and ROTCE expectations as speaker at Goldman Sachs 2023 U.S. Financial Services Conference. Based on Mason's update, expect Q4 EPS of $.23 ($1.26 excluding FDIC and restructuring charges), and full year EPS of $5.46 ($6.04 excluding FDIC, restructuring charges and divestitures). Mason made it clear that revenues must grow 4% - 5% a year, and expenses must be cut by $1B - $2B a year to reach ROTCE of 11%-12%.

title: Citigroup's massive restructuring to cost $1B, won't be completed until next quarter
text: Citigroup Chief Financial Officer Mark Mason said Wednesday the bank's largest reorganization in decades will cost about $1 billion for charges related to restructuring and severance.

title: Wall Street CEOs say proposed banking rules will hurt small businesses, low-income Americans
text: The heads of America's largest banks, including JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America and Goldman Sachs are seeking to dull the impact of the new rules.

title: Citigroup: Presents Technical Strength At Support Levels
text: Citigroup reported steady net income and stable earnings per share, supported by a 9% increase in revenue. Services and Markets divisions of the Institutional Clients Group and U.S. Personal Banking drive revenue growth. Technical analysis suggests a bullish outlook for Citigroup's stock, with potential for an upward breakout above $52.

title: Wall Street bank bosses warn lawmakers of economic toll from tough new rules
text: Wall Street bank bosses warned lawmakers on the Senate Banking Committee on Wednesday that capital hikes and other new regulations being contemplated by U.S. bank regulators will hurt lending, capital markets and the broader economy.

title: Mortgages and small loans will be more expensive under proposed regulations, bank chiefs say
text: The chief executives of big U.S. banks are arguing that banking regulations aimed at strengthening the country's financial system will instead hurt the economy.

title: Citi's 2023 revenue expected to be at lower end of forecast, CFO says
text: Citigroup's full-year revenue is expected to be towards the lower end of its previous forecast, at around $78 billion, Chief Financial Officer Mark Mason said at a conference on Wednesday.

title: Bank Stocks Are Heating Up as Tech Stocks Falter. How to Play the Trend.
text: Bank stocks are starting to show some signs of life relative to the broader S&P 500.  I've had a few guests on my Lead-Lag Live podcast who have noted that it looks like a good setup for financial stocks here.

title: Citi, BofA left with some Barclays stock after Qatari stake sale –sources
text: Citigroup and Bank of America were left holding some shares in Barclays after they could not identify enough demand for a 510 million pound ($642 million) stake sold by Qatar Holding, people familiar with the matter told Reuters.

title: U.S. bank CEOs expected to protest regulation push before Congress
text: The top bosses of JPMorgan, Bank of America, Citigroup, Wells Fargo and other major banks are expected to warn lawmakers this week that capital hikes and other new regulations will hurt the economy and should be indefinitely shelved.
